title: Handoff kinds + guarded writer/adopter verbs (lib + CLI, RED-first)
action: Ship the two-kind handoff lifecycle as lib + CLI (per D1; additive per D4; claim-next selection is fsh-11's — NOT here). state.mjs: (1) readHandoff stays fail-open for display but gains normalization: kind is 'planned-next' | 'pause'; a missing or unknown kind NORMALIZES TO 'pause' (fail-safe: every legacy handoff keeps surface-and-wait semantics). (2) writeHandoff (strict): planned-next REFUSES (typed) unless the named previous cell is capped with trace.verify_passed === true (the capCell precondition field, see cells.mjs) AND the named next cell's claim file exists and is owned by the writing session (the carried claim — D1's preconditions live in the verb, not prose); the record ALSO stores writer_session (the writing session id — fsh-10's source-gating consumes it, panel W1); pause handoffs keep today's free-form fields plus the kind; no partial writes. (3) adoptHandoff(sessionId): for a planned-next record, adopts the carried claim via claims.mjs adoptClaim (gate-based, claim file continuously present), then clears the handoff — PRECISION (panel W5): this is CLEAR-AFTER-ADOPT with idempotent recovery, not a transaction; a crash between the two steps self-heals on the next adoptHandoff call (benign self-adopt then clear) — document exactly that in the function comment, never claim atomicity across the two files. On adoption failure returns the typed refusal (never a throw), leaving both claim and handoff intact. (4) CLI verbs via the unified registry, one entry + one handler each (handlers live in bee.mjs's HANDLERS map — fsh-4 pattern) with an exercised runExample row per entry (runExample harness in test_bee_cli.mjs ~:116): state.handoff.write / state.handoff.adopt / state.handoff.show. Zero-handoff and no-session repos: every existing row passes unmodified. RED-first with RED evidence. Vendor byte-identical siblings (cmp chain in verify).

truths: A planned-next handoff cannot be written when the previous cell is uncapped, its verify_passed is not true, or the carried claim is absent/not owned by the writer — each refusal typed, no partial write
A planned-next record carries writer_session; a handoff with a missing or unknown kind behaves as pause everywhere (fail-safe for every legacy record)

Adoption transfers the carried claim to the adopting session and then clears the handoff; a crash between the steps is recovered idempotently by the next adopt call; a failed adoption leaves both claim and handoff intact with a typed refusal
Every existing suite row passes unmodified (zero-handoff/no-session byte-parity)

artifacts.substantive: schema'd two-kind handoff with guarded writer/adopter, writer_session, typed refusals, clear-after-adopt with documented idempotent recovery

key_links: adoptHandoff rides claims.mjs adoptClaim (gate-based); every new verb has an exercised runExample row
prohibitions: No weakened or deleted existing assertion

verify: node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs && cmp -s skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/state.mjs .bee/bin/lib/state.mjs && cmp -s skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/claims.mjs .bee/bin/lib/claims.mjs && cmp -s skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/command-registry.mjs .bee/bin/lib/command-registry.mjs && cmp -s skills/bee-hive/templates/bee.mjs .bee/bin/bee.mjs

trace.outcome: Two-kind handoff lifecycle (planned-next | pause, D1) shipped as lib + CLI: readHandoff normalizes kind (missing/unknown -> pause, fail-safe); writeHandoff is the strict guarded writer (planned-next refuses, zero mutation, unless the previous cell is capped with trace.verify_passed true AND the next cell's claim is owned by writer_session; record stores writer_session/previous_cell/next_cell); adoptHandoff wraps claims.mjs adoptClaim with clear-after-adopt + idempotent self-heal recovery, typed refusal never a throw. Three new CLI verbs (state.handoff.write/adopt/show) via the unified registry with exercised runExample rows. RED-first (12+6 genuine failures captured via git-stash against the new tests) then green: test_lib.mjs 275/0, test_bee_cli.mjs 120/0, test_onboard_bee.mjs PASS, 4-way cmp vendored parity all pass.
